title: Campanopsis oxyphylla

It belongs to the order Ericales, an order of flowering plants that includes a variety of woody and herbaceous species. At the rank of genus, Campanopsis oxyphylla is part of the monotypic genus Campanopsis, which contains only a single species. The species is native to China and is often found growing in shaded, mountainous areas. As a member of the Diapensiaceae family, Campanopsis oxyphylla is closely related to other plants in this family, such as the diaspensia and shortia species.

Its roots are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems.
